---
title: "data_wrangle_pt2"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# Averaging quarterly data into annual data.

library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
# This section takes the EU data, cleans it up, and turns it into annual data.
raw_gdp <- read.csv("data/API_NY.GDP.PCAP.CD_DS2_en_csv_v2_46.csv", header = TRUE, na.strings = "")
countries <- c("Austria", "Belgium", "Bulgaria", "Croatia", "Cyrpus", "Czechia", "Denmark", "Estonia", "Finland", "France", "Germany", "Greece", "Hungary", "Ireland", "Italy", "Latvia", "Lithuania", "Luxembourg", "Malta", "Netherlands", "Poland", "Portugal", "Romania", "Slovak Republic", "Slovenia", "Spain", "Sweden")
EU_gdps <- raw_gdp %>%
    filter(Country.Name %in% countries) %>%
    select(-Country.Code, -Indicator.Name, -Indicator.Code ) %>%
    gather(key = "year", value = "index", X1960:X)
    

sumtable <- EU_gdps %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
      summarize(avr_gdp =
              mean(index, na.rm = TRUE))
colnames(EU_gdps) <- tolower(colnames(EU_gdps))
 sumtable$year <- gsub("X", "", sumtable$year)

summary(EU_gdps)

raw_labor <- read.csv("data/ECB_Data Portal_20260316215613.csv", header = TRUE)


raw_labor$TIME.PERIOD <- gsub("Q\\d", "", raw_labor$TIME.PERIOD)

sumlabor <- raw_labor %>%
  group_by(TIME.PERIOD) %>%
    summarize(quarter_avr =
                mean(Labour.Productivity..per.hours.worked...index..MNA.Q.Y.I9.W0.S1.S1._Z.LPR_HW._Z.OTQ._Z.IX.LR.N.))

#Merging GDP and Productivity for both

sumlabor <- setnames(sumlabor, "TIME.PERIOD", "year")

EU_total <- left_join(sumlabor, sumtable)




# Cleanup for fredgraph.csv. Turning data points into annual data.

raw_fred <- read.csv("data/fredgraph.csv")

raw_fred$observation_date <- substring(raw_fred$observation_date, 1, 4)

sum_fred <- setnames(raw_fred, "observation_date", "year")

sum_fred <- setnames(raw_fred, "COMPNFB_CPIAUCSL", "compensation")

sum_fred <- setnames(raw_fred, "OPHNFB_NBD19700101", "productivity")

fred_annual <- sum_fred %>%
    group_by(year) %>%
    summarize(compensation = mean(compensation), productivity = mean(productivity))


# Tie it allll together
compare_total <- left_join(EU_total, fred_annual)

dir.create("data", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
write.csv(compare_total, file = file.path("data", "EUdata.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```
